name: Ascent to the Rodana del Pico via the Balsa Barreta — circular from Riba-roja de Túria
name_original: Subida a la Rodana del Pico por la Balsa Barreta — circular desde Riba-roja de Túria
region_intro: Hiking around Riba-Roja De Túria offers diverse landscapes within the Turia Natural Park, a significant green space near Valencia. The region is characterized by the Turia River, forming a green corridor with rich riverside flora, alongside mountainous areas featuring pine woods like Les Rodanes. These natural features provide varied terrain for exploration, from gentle river paths to more elevated routes. The area's geology and vegetation contribute to a varied hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Riba-Roja De Túria The most popular hiking route is Túria Natural Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route traverses diverse landscapes along the Turia River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the Rodana del Pico via the Balsa Barreta — circular from Riba-roja de Túria, a moderate 9.6 miles (15.4 km) path. This trail leads through pine woods and features notable elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Parque Natural del Túria— circular by the Embalse de la Vallesa and the poblado íbero de la Lloma De Betxí, a 11.6 miles (18.7 km) trail leading through the Turia Natural Park, often completed in about 4 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around Riba-Roja De Túria is defined by the Turia River's green corridor, pine woods of Les Rodanes, and varied terrain within the Turia Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to moderate climbs. The routes in Riba-Roja De Túria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 500 hikers have used komoot to explore Riba-Roja De Túria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: There are over 40 hiking routes around Riba-Roja de Túria, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences within the Turia Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking routes in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: Yes, Riba-Roja de Túria offers 12 easy routes suitable for families and beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Túria River Fluvial loop from Urbanització Mas de Traver&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 7.3 km path that follows the river, perfect for a relaxed outing. The "A Route through the Turia Natural Park" is also mentioned as a family-friendly, low-difficulty 5 km circular option starting at the Old Bridge.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails in Riba-Roja de Túria are rich in natural beauty. You can explore the lush riverside flora along the Turia River, discover pine woods in areas like Les Rodanes, and even find the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4058748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Galleç Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is part of the Turia Natural Park, known for its diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Riba-Roja de Túria are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375004303/ascent-to-the-rodana-del-pico-via-the-balsa-barreta-circular-from-riba-roja-de-turia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Rodana del Pico via the Balsa Barreta — circular from Riba-roja de Túria&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1374850163/parque-natural-del-turia-circular-by-the-embalse-de-la-vallesa-and-the-poblado-ibero-de-la-lloma-de-betxi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural del Túria— circular by the Embalse de la Vallesa and the poblado íbero de la Lloma De Betxí&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Túria River Fluvial loop from Urbanització Mas de Traver&lt;/a&gt; is another easy circular option.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous areas within the Turia Natural Park, particularly Les Rodanes, offer routes that lead to elevated points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1085458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rodana Gran&lt;/a&gt;, providing impressive panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2674584" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carasoles Geodetic Point&lt;/a&gt; is another notable viewpoint.

Q: What historical or cultural sites can be explored while hiking?
A: Riba-Roja de Túria integrates history into its natural landscapes. You can encounter remnants of coastal machinery and Spanish Civil War trenches along routes like PR-CV 175 Les Rodanes. The Turia River paths also lead to cultural points such as the Pont Vell (Old Bridge) and the Castle of Riba-roja. The Parque Natural del Túria circular route passes by the Iberian settlement of La Lloma de Betxí.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with milder temperatures. However, the diverse landscapes, especially the shaded riverside paths, can offer pleasant conditions even during warmer months. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any trails that feature unique bridges or structures?
A: Yes, the Turia river course features an engaging 27 km route punctuated by 19 &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1066613" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridges of Parc Natural del Túria&lt;/a&gt;, offering a unique and entertaining way to experience the river. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1375104537/turia-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Túria Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; also features the Iron Bridge.

Q: What kind of flora and fauna can I observe on the trails?
A: The Turia Natural Park is an oasis of biodiversity. Along the Turia River, you'll find exceptional riverside flora including willows, elms, black poplars, and ashes. The mountainous areas are adorned with Mediterranean forests. Hikers can observe local flora and fauna, making it a great spot for nature enthusiasts.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil Turia River corridor to the pine woods of Les Rodanes, and the variety of routes suitable for different abilities.

Q: Is parking available at trailheads in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: While specific parking locations for every trailhead are not detailed, Riba-Roja de Túria is well-equipped for outdoor visitors. It's advisable to check specific route descriptions on komoot for information on designated parking areas or public transport access points near your chosen starting point.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Riba-Roja de Túria?
A: Many natural parks and outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, especially on trails where dogs are kept on a leash. The Turia Natural Park is generally welcoming to dogs. However, always check specific regulations for each trail or protected area to ensure compliance and respect for wildlife.
tips: Top of Rodana del Pico with very nice views. It is part of the Paraje Natural de Les Rodanes, a very picturesque site where forests and groves prevail just a few kilometers outside the Valencia metropolitan area.
Raft that formerly fulfilled the function of collecting rainwater and providing water for the cattle that were raised in that area.
